case_id: USLCI FY24 Q4 or FY25 Q1 (JSON-LD format)

pi_items.source: Task description line 4 specifies '~/LCA_Results/circular_pet_result.csv', which expands to /home/ga/LCA_Results/circular_pet_result.csv
Task description step 2 explicitly lists these three process names
Task description step 1 states: 'Create necessary flows: rPET Flake, Scrap Bottle, and 50% rPET Bottle.' The metadata omits the third required flow.

Web search confirms polyethylene terephthalate is the chemical name for virgin PET resin. USLCI database (from NREL/ERG) includes virgin PET material data produced from terephthalic acid and ethylene glycol precursors.

Task description step 4 specifies 'Run an LCIA calculation using TRACI 2.1 (GWP/Global Warming)'. Web search confirms TRACI 2.1 is a specific EPA LCIA method released in 2012 with multiple impact categories including Global Warming Potential.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real LCA data: USLCI database (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory from NREL/FLCAC, downloaded from GitHub) and TRACI v2.1 LCIA methods (from Figshare, based on EPA methodology). USLCI contains real gate-to-gate and cradle-to-grave inventory data for virgin polyethylene terephthalate (PET) and other industrial processes. The task requires three processes (Bottle Manufacturing, Use and Collection, Recycling Operations) and involves calculating Global Warming Potential using TRACI 2.1. One metadata discrepancy: the required_flows list is incomplete—the task description states three flows are needed ('rPET Flake', 'Scrap Bottle', '50% rPET Bottle') but metadata only lists two.
